semantic.role: Pipeline task module that reconciles the internal manifest state and file cache with the filesystem, ensuring language indices and file metadata are consistent.
responsibilities: Load the current language snapshot from the manifest to determine available language extensions.
Detect runtime-tracked extensions via LangRegistry and filter scanned files to those with relevant extensions.
Read existing index documents from the filesystem, verifying compatibility and reporting issues.
Calculate file hashes and determine whether the manifest's file statuses need updating based on actual file contents.
Update the manifest's file statuses (tracking, hash, etc.) and clear stale export data for files that no longer exist or are out of sync.
outOfScope: CLI command parsing or argument handling.
Service orchestration beyond the pipeline stage.
Direct database access (delegated to manifest/repository abstractions).
invariants: Task modules must implement stage-local work on top of core contracts and engines, avoiding CLI command parsing or unrelated orchestration (rule `task-stage-boundary`).
Only files with extensions tracked by the language registry or explicit compatible types (.md, .json) are processed.
Index documents must pass compatibility checks (`isCompatibleIndexDocument`) before their contents are used.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: This task implements the reconcile stage of the ArchSpine pipeline, ensuring that the manifest's representation of files and their metadata matches the actual filesystem state.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Initial creation of the ReconcileTask as part of the pipeline infrastructure, per the initial commit of the repository.

publicSurface.description: Pipeline stage task that reconciles the manifest's file state and cached indexes with the filesystem. It scans files, verifies language index compatibility, updates file statuses, and clears stale exports. Consumed by the task runner as one of the pipeline steps.
